what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request item that proposes a 'DRAG BUILD' experiment: running concurrent, independent build attempts from a shared grounded blueprint/evidence pack, then comparing and synthesizing the outputs without auto-merging or declaring a winner.
observations: It frames a build-side analogue to evidence dragnets.
It outlines a candidate experiment with 3-6 isolated build attempts using different models, sessions, or strategies.
Possible outputs include code prototypes, tests, interface/data models, migration plans, API shapes, registry/schema proposals, docs, alternative UI models, and implementation risk maps.
Boundaries require reversibility and forbid source overwrites, deletes, deployments, auth/security changes, and external publishing unless explicitly approved.
It calls for a non-opinionated comparison across changes, assumptions, test results, omissions, risks, and reusable parts.
It is a request type item in state open, sourced from claude_code, in the provider-registry channel.
It explicitly separates fact, inference, and possibility.
Tim is named as the human decision maker for unresolved semantic judgement.
The file uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history.
standouts: Proposes a structured, multi-model concurrent build experiment as the build-side counterpart to a dragnet.
Explicitly forbids auto-merge, winner declaration, source overwrites, or deployment without approval.
Requires a synthesis record that separates evidence, candidates, tradeoffs, unresolved semantic decisions, and recommended next exploration.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, state, and history.
Markdown body with hierarchical headings and bullet lists.
Follows the noticeboard request item format in channel-memory.
Neutral, procedural tone with clear boundaries and purpose sections.
concerns: Terms like 'DRAG BUILD' and 'dragnet' are not defined inside the file, so their precise meaning depends on channel context.
The candidate experiment mixes model-specific attempts with role-based attempts without specifying how isolation and addressability are enforced.
No concrete blueprint or target is named, and success criteria remain high-level.
History contains only the initial filing event despite an updated timestamp matching created.
notes: A request to trial concurrent, reversible build attempts from a shared blueprint and synthesize them without merging or ranking.
questions: What is the precise definition of a 'dragnet' and 'DRAG BUILD' in this channel?
Which concrete blueprint or target will be used for the first experiment?
How will candidates be stored, branched, or addressed technically?
What tooling will enforce the reversibility and isolation boundaries?
Who performs the comparison/synthesis passes and how are conflicts resolved?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to discover whether concurrent model capacity can produce diverse grounded implementation candidates from the same blueprint; the file does that by laying out an experiment plan, boundaries, and expected outputs, so purpose and actual content align.
apparent_themes: Concurrent, independent artifact generation from shared evidence.
Separation of evidence, inference, and possibility.
intention_signals: It is trying to extend dragnet-style parallel evidence gathering into the build phase, creating an addressable, comparable set of grounded implementation candidates that can be inspected and composed rather than blindly merged.
novelty: The build-side analogue to a dragnet — explicitly treating code/design construction as a parallel, reversible, multi-model candidate-generation process with a separate synthesis pass.
contribution: It contributes a structured experimental protocol for concurrent build attempts, including isolation, reversibility, comparison dimensions, and synthesis outputs, that a fused system can keep as a reusable template for multi-model build exploration.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard request item proposing a 'DRAG BUILD' experiment to generate multiple isolated, reversible implementation candidates from a shared blueprint using concurrent models/sessions, then compare and synthesize them without auto-merging.
intention_for_embedding: To define a safe, structured way to test whether parallel model capacity can produce diverse grounded build artifacts from the same design plan for later human judgement and composition.
